text.content:
The City of Pigs

A Scenario inspired by The Three Little Pigs (Joseph Jacobs)

Safety Keywords: Body horror / Worker exploitation / Prostitution / Blasphemy

Once upon a time...

The busy town of Whitehaven, under the leadership of wealthy industrialist Bardolf Greed, is experiencing incredible expansion with investments in large coal mines and textile factories. The rich magnate has spared no expense in his pursuit of profit, and cares nothing for the wretched workers whose sweat built his empire.

Whitehaven is devastated by acts of violence and sabotage, which have interfered with its industrial productivity considerably. People are afraid to walk the streets because a criminal gang, called the Pig Heads, goes around kidnapping the most skilled and capable workers. From under the Mine, a rebellion is brewing...

The story that led...

Bardolf Greed is a wealthy industrialist who made his fortune thanks to shrewd deals and trade. Reaching the ripe old age of seventy without an heir, Bardolf has decided to dedicate his life to the only true, great love of his life: money.

Too old to travel, Bardolf has moved to Whitehaven, an unremarkable town whose only resource was the rich mines beneath the city. Within a few years, Bardolf transformed Whitehaven into an industrial city by trapping all its inhabitants into employment with starvation wages.

The Browne Brothers

But not all of Whitehaven's citizens were willing to give in to the wealthy magnate. The brothers Clarence, Frank, and Arthur Browne have always cared for the community. Arthur, in particular, worked for years to make Whitehaven a quiet town where every resident could have a home and a job to get by.

Bardolf, however, has bought the town's favor, seizing property and leveling houses to build both factories and dormitories for the now homeless workers. By falsifying some official papers, Bardolf managed to throw the three brothers out on the street, forcing them to accept a job as miners or starve.

Clarence soon quit, choosing the life of a slacker over labor in the mines. Frank and Arthur's time in the mines was ended by a major mine collapse, during which Frank lost an arm and a leg, and Arthur was trapped by falling rubble and left for dead.

The God Under the Mine

The collapse was caused by the awakening of The God Under the Mine, a huge pig-like creature embedded in the rock below the city. Alive and trapped underground, Arthur found the God's head and after days of agony he fed on divine flesh, discovering that it confers incredible powers.

Crazed with grief and a wild hope that Arthur wasn't dead, Frank dug a long vertical tunnel, eventually managing to create a communication channel with Arthur. United in vengeance and endowed with the power of divine flesh, the three brothers then began their personal war against Bardolf Greed.

The Pig Heads

From under the Mine, Arthur leads the rebellion by passing pieces of the quickly regenerating God's flesh to Clarence and Frank. Frank has managed to get hired as a butcher at the big meat factory in the city and secretly mixes the flesh of the God with common meat, infecting the unsuspecting miners who eat it.

When the mutation begins to take effect, Clarence approaches the targets, offering them the opportunity to join the gang. Bardolf senses that the city is under threat, but does not imagine that his enemy is someone he already hates: Arthur, left for dead after the accident. With over fifty Pig Heads now working in the mines, Arthur counts the days before he can launch his personal revolution.

pages.name: The Story at the Arrival of the Hunters

text.content:
The story at the arrival of the Hunters...

Whitehaven is devastated by acts of violence and sabotage, which have interfered with its industrial productivity considerably. People are afraid to walk the streets because a criminal gang, called the Pig Heads, goes around kidnapping the most skilled and capable workers.

Bardolf Greed has called in the Hunters to investigate and stop the Pig Heads before they destroy everything he has built. The wealthy industrialist makes it clear that productivity is everything—if forced to choose, Hunters should put the preservation of property and machinery before the lives of the townspeople.

Main NPCs

Bardolf Greed - The Wealthy Industrialist

OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a wealthy and skilled businessman who always knows how to find a good deal, the one that turns a profit for me. My body is old, but my mind remains infallible.

Agenda: Own every business and aspect of trade in Whitehaven, so that the whole city is under my total control.

Gift - Smell the Deal: Bardolf is a master of negotiation and reading others. After talking to a subject, Bardolf can obtain one of the following pieces of information: 1. Discover a secret regarding the person he is speaking with; 2. Obtain an object or piece of information valuable to the other party in the negotiation, and which he can use as leverage. A Hunter can make a Defense Check to prevent Bardolf from using this Gift on them.

Arthur Browne - The Big Brother

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am the eldest of the Pig Heads and act as the mastermind of the gang. Living in my natural prison has taught me how to see even in total darkness.

Agenda: Take revenge on Bardolf for corrupting everything I built in Whitehaven and regain control of the city / Transforming all the inhabitants of Whitehaven into Pig Heads.

Gift - The Flesh of The God Under the Mine: Arthur can tear pieces of flesh from the God's head to harness its powers. When he spends an action, the piece becomes a Spawn of the God and begins to grow disgustingly, engaging all nearby enemies. Arthur can tear one piece of meat off per action, up to a maximum of 3.

Gift - Gang Leader: Eating the flesh of The God Under the Mine gives the Browne brothers the ability to transform into monstrous beings. Once per Scene, the NPC can mutate, gaining the following benefits: 1. Recover a Wound once they reach 0. Though apparently dead, the NPC will revive within minutes of falling; 2. Once per Scene, act a second time during the Exchange, even consecutively; 3. Once per Scene, increase the Opposition Level by 2 for a single action where they can harness their superhuman strength.

Frank Browne - The Middle Brother

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (4 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am the middleman of the Pig Heads and act as the strongarm of the gang. I have control over all cultists, and the power of The God Under the Mine has given me strength and endurance equal to that of five men.

Agenda: Take revenge on Bardolf for what he did to me and find a remedy for the wounds that have marred me since the day of the accident / Mix the flesh of The God Under the Mine with beef from the slaughterhouse and feed it to the miners to add to the ranks of the gang.

Gift - The Pincer: Frank replaced his left arm with a home-made mechanical arm with many functions. During a Scene, Frank can use each of the following one time: 1. Crush, a hit with the pincer inflicts 1 additional Wound; 2. Pulley, the pincer can launch from the arm and raise or lower Frank about thirty feet. Frank can carry 2 other subjects with him.

Gift - Gang Leader: Same as Arthur above.

Clarence Browne - The Younger Brother

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am the youngest of the Pig Heads and, despite believing in our fight, I am not going to waste much time on it. From hanging around, I know Whitehaven like the back of my hand.

Agenda: Having fun and lazing around all day, carrying on the Pig Head's cause in my spare time.

Gift - Slacker: Clarence is a master of the art of disappearing when things get tough. In the presence of even a small crowd, Clarence can leave a Scene when it is his turn to act, without anything or anyone being able to stop him. When using this Gift, Clarence hides in the orchard, passing the entire following Scene there.

Gift - Gang Leader: Same as Arthur above.

Martha Browne - The Mother

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am the mother of three brothers, and I am so old that I have almost completely lost my sight. My son Frank takes care of me, but he doesn't tell me anything. I am convinced that Arthur is not dead, for my instinct as a mother tells me so.

Emma - The Childhood Friend

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a childhood friend of Clarence and have a special relationship with him. He has always promised that he will take me away from this place, but I know that he will never abandon his brothers. I understand that he has a big secret, but tries to protect me by keeping me in the dark.

Threats and Creatures

The Pig Heads

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: We are a band of revolutionaries in the service of the three brothers. The God's flesh gives us power and fills us with the anger we need to wage our war.

Gift – Pigs' Gang: When they act, each member of the gang changes to become stronger. Their head becomes that of a pig and only another Pig Head will be able to communicate with them. Their mutation grants a random power, roll 1 die:

  1. Strength, able to inflict 1 additional Wound once per Scene
  2. Speed, once per Scene acts 2 times consecutively
  3. Resistance, +1 Wound
  4. Abomination, +1 to Opposition Level
  5. Grunt, once per Scene can emit a loud squeal, enemies who hear it receive 1 Penalty on their next action
  6. Out of control, the OL rises to Hard 7 but, at the end of their action, the NPC explodes into a cloud of flesh and blood

Bardolf's Guards

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: We are long-time mercenaries, paid handsomely by Bardolf for our loyalty.

William - The Man of Law

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a prominent lawyer and I know all of Bardolf's secrets. I manage the accounts of both the Factories and mine with zealous attention.

Edward - The Miners' Chief

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: I am a long-time friend of Arthur and mourn his death. Everyone in the mine does what I say, and I hate foreigners who want to poke their nose into my work. I allow Frank into the mine often to visit the place where Arthur died.

The God Under the Mine

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (6 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a creature as old as the world, trapped beneath the bedrock. Millennia of inactivity have made me catatonic and there is nothing that can really wake me up. Only pain can stir me even slightly.

Gift - The Head of the God: Only the enormous head of the God emerges from the rock, and pieces of meat can be obtained from it. When he suffers 1 Wound, the God emits a loud grunt that sends everyone flying (a Defense Check can avoid this). As an additional effect of the grunt, the God's fetid breath taints the air, granting 1 Penalty to the next action of all affected subjects, excluding Arthur. When reduced to 0 Wounds, the God falls into a deep sleep to fully regenerate in the following Scene.

Spawn of The God Under the Mine

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: An abomination of flesh and tentacles, obeying Arthur's will.

Gift – Flesh Abomination: A Spawn can perform its action with a flesh tentacle up to a range of 6 feet, and at the end of each Exchange it will grow as indicated in the growth table. If not engaged in a conflict, the Spawn will grow 1 step per Scene. When the Spawn acts, roll 1 die: from 1 to 5 it will rage against one of Arthur's enemies chosen at random. On a 6, it will act against an ally or Arthur himself. If the Spawn has more actions, roll the die for each single action.

Growth Table:

  • Step 1: +1 Wound, Range of 12 feet
  • Step 2: +1 Action, +1 OL. If a target fails a Defense Check, in addition to suffering 1 Wound it will be dragged towards the Spawn or thrown away
  • Step 3: +1 Wound, +1 OL. Mass growth to a size of 18 cubic feet

Key Locations

Greed's Mansion

Descriptor: A small castle in the center of Whitehaven. The pomp of each room underlines Greed's incredible wealth.

Whitehaven

Descriptor: An industrial city growing around a large coal mine. The noise and fumes from the factories fill the air at every hour of the day, while groups of workers go back and forth waiting for their shift.

Random Encounters (roll 1d6):

  1. 1-2: They meet Clarence, who will try to pump the Hunters for information by pretending to be well connected.
  2. 3-4: They meet Emma, who offers her services and is curious to talk to foreigners.
  3. 5-6: A group of Pig Heads equal to double the number of Hunters set up an ambush.

The Factories

Descriptor: A series of shoddy, stone buildings used as textile factories and foundries. Access is forbidden to outsiders.

Information from Workers (roll 1d6):

  1. 1-2: Everyone aspires to work at the slaughterhouse where the shifts are less pressing, but the unpleasant Frank Browne selects the staff. Frank ignores even the most piteous plea and no one understands how he chooses who can work alongside him.
  2. 3-4: Just outside the city there is an apple orchard that once employed many inhabitants of Whitehaven. Now it is just a place where slackers like Clarence Browne, as well as those who are unable to work in the factories or in the mine, take refuge.
  3. 5-6: Not long ago, there was a collapse in the mines that injured or killed many miners. Arthur Browne, a prominent citizen who did much for the city before Greed's arrival, was one of the victims.

The Slaughterhouse

Descriptor: A huge gray building inside which cattle from coastal pastures are slaughtered. Entry is forbidden to non-workers.

Areas (roll 1d6 for progress, or present in sequence):

  1. 1-2 The outdoor garden: It is more an open-air barn than a garden patch. Bloodstains and excrement suggest that the slaughter starts here. Several signs reading "No Trespassing" suggest that the area is very controlled.
  2. 3-4 The slaughterhouse: Composed of a series of large spaces, several stairs lead to underground chambers where the meat is preserved. Around ten workers are present day and night, all members of the Pig Heads.
  3. 5-6 The salting room: A small room at the back of the building, where Frank prepares the meat that will be served in the cafeterias throughout the city. During the day Frank is always present, intent on hiding the flesh of the God within freshly butchered beef.

The Orchard

Descriptor: A large apple orchard which, before the arrival of the factories, was one of the main sources of work in the city. Now, however, it is abandoned and represents a home for all those who cannot get a job.

Areas (roll 1d6, or present in sequence):

  1. 1-2 The orchard: The trees have been neglected and on the ground there are still abandoned tools and baskets for harvesting apples. A sign next to a tool shed reads "Gift courtesy of the Browne family".
  2. 3-4 Clarence's shack: A rickety rundown house with a thatched roof. Inside, it is possible to find a rusty key that says "slaughterhouse" and a sketched map of the mines that says "well". Around the hut there are always 1d6 + 2 Pig Heads.
  3. 5-6 Clarence: The youngest of the Browne brothers is lying on the ground, visibly drunk, under an apple tree. Clarence always pretends to be drunk with strangers to take them off guard.

The Mine

Descriptor: A gigantic coal mine where miners work day and night. Many areas are abandoned in favor of more profitable veins.

Gift - Maze of Galleries: Whitehaven's Mine is gigantic and without a guide it's not easy to get to a specific location. With each exploration roll, you can get 1 or more Map Tokens which represent progress in learning the layout of the mine. Upon reaching 3 Map Tokens, you can go directly to the Special result.

Exploration Results (roll 1d6 or choose):

  1. 1-2 Work zone A: Threading through the tunnels, deafened by the sound of steam engines and digging, the Hunters encounter a group of miners (2d6 + 6 Minor NPCs). If the Hunters do something weird, one of the workers will alert Edward. Exploration will yield 1 Map Token.
  2. 3-4 Work zone B: Threading through the tunnels, deafened by the sound of steam engines and digging, the Hunters encounter a group of miners (2d6 + 6 Minor NPCs). If the Hunters do something weird, 1d6 + 6 miners will reveal themselves as Pig Heads and attack. Exploration will yield 2 Map Tokens.
  3. 5-6 Abandoned lodging: In an area close to the collapse, recent traces of passage can be found. The Hunters meet Frank and 5 Pig Heads who are going to the well to meet up with Arthur. Exploration will yield 2 Map Tokens.
  4. Special (3 Map Tokens): By moving a heavy stone, the Hunters will find the mouth of the well that leads to Arthur's chamber. The shaft is narrow and thirty feet deep, but with a little attention a person can descend it. Climbing down the pit can alert Arthur.

The Cave of The God Under the Mine

Descriptor: A large natural cave, which Arthur has made into a home. You can enter only by descending from a narrow well. In the north wall, the enormous pig head of the God gasps and groans as it waits to wake up.

Gift - Arthur's House: The mastermind behind the Pig Heads worked hard to make the Cave an intruder-proof haven. If someone drops from the well, Arthur will be alerted (a Position Check can avoid this). At the bottom of the well is a large tub that Arthur can fill with boiling water. Whoever arrives through the well suffers 1 Wound if they have no way of avoiding the hot water. Inside the room, Arthur can turn the lights on and off thanks to a system he has invented, throwing the chamber into either light or darkness. While intruders wander blindly, Arthur can use his knowledge of the place to move freely.

Scenario Events

In this Scenario, there are eight Events. As a Storyteller, feel free to create new ones in response to the actions of the Hunters.

If the supply of infected meat is not stopped: After two days from the arrival of the Hunters the Pig Heads will number over one hundred and Arthur will give the order to attack the city.

If the Hunters discover that Arthur is still alive and express any doubts about the three brothers: The shrewd money maker will give full access to the mine and its maps. With them, it will be possible to calculate the exact location of The God Under the Mine. If the Hunters commit to Bardolf in his presence, the industrialist will set up a defense with a dozen guards at his mansion, using himself as bait to lure in Arthur.

If the Hunters make their way to the mine and outside the city: Clarence will attempt to assassinate Bardolf in his palace, but will fail and end up on the run, chased by the guards after being injured.

If Clarence or Frank know about it and the Hunters talk to Bardolf about the three brothers: Bardolf will call the Hunters in to report, describing a Pig Head who was acting alone and who is now wounded and therefore recognizable.

After the Hunters commit to one side: The other side will attempt to eliminate them. Frank will set up a defense with a dozen Pig Heads, and Arthur will help his brother, passing him pieces of meat to create several Spawn of the God.

After Arthur creates the third Spawn in the same Scene: The God Under the Mine goes mad with pain and tries to move, causing a new earthquake. The mine collapses, overwhelming everyone inside, though the Hunters may find a quick way to escape.

Exclusive Scenario Gifts

These Gifts represent special abilities related to the The City of Pigs Scenario and can be acquired once the requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one.

The Meat of The God Under the Mine

Requirement: Obtain a piece of the flesh of the God at the slaughterhouse or defeat a Spawn.

You can consume the flesh of the God to temporarily gain power. Once per Scene, you can heal 1 Wound and gain 1 Additional Success for all physical actions you take. You have to wait an entire Scene for the remaining flesh to regenerate. If you use this Gift in 2 consecutive Scenes, the flesh is consumed and you won't be able to use it anymore.

The Brand of Greed

Requirement: To gain the trust and respect of Bardolf Greed.

Bardolf trusts only those who demonstrate cunning and ability, granting such people a special pass called The Brand of Greed. With the Brand you get free access to any place in the city, without the person in charge being able to object to your entrance. Get 1 Additional Success when you exploit the Mark's importance against a city dweller.

There Is No House That Can Stop Me (Special Gift for Garou the Old Wolf)

Requirement: Clash with one of the brothers who has made use of the Leader of the Gang of Pigs Gift.

Those damned pigs get on your nerves and you can't stand them running away from your wrath. You get 2 Additional Successes when you take action to destroy or overcome an environmental obstacle that stands between you and one of the three Pigs at the head of the gang. You hate to lose to them. If you have to leave or flee from a place without eliminating them, you lose 1 Soma.

The Scenario in Scenes

FIRST SCENE – The Interview with Bardolf Greed: The Hunters meet with wealthy industrialist Bardolf Greed in his opulent dining room. Bardolf explains that a gang of criminals called the Pig Heads is kidnapping factory workers and sabotaging productivity. He asks the Hunters to investigate and stop them, making it clear that property and machinery are more important than workers' lives. From Bardolf, the Hunters learn about: the Pig Heads wearing grotesque pig masks, the Browne family's opposition to the factories, Edward Right controlling the mine, Frank Browne running the slaughterhouse, and that productivity must be preserved above all.

SECOND SCENE – Visit to the City: Exploring Whitehaven's alleys and factories, the Hunters gather information from workers about the slaughterhouse (where Frank selects workers mysteriously), the abandoned orchard (refuge for slackers like Clarence), and Arthur Browne's death in the mine collapse. As night falls, a group of Pig Heads ambushes the Hunters. After the fight, the Hunters notice one attacker wore a butcher's coat and another dressed as a beggar—clues pointing to the slaughterhouse and orchard.

THIRD SCENE OPTIONS: The Hunters must investigate the sources of the Pig Heads.

  • OPTION A – The Slaughterhouse: Hunters explore the massive slaughterhouse, finding the outdoor garden with blood and "No Trespassing" signs, the main floor with ten Pig Head workers, and finally the salting room where Frank is caught mixing the God's flesh with meat for the miners. Frank fights to the death. Searching the room reveals flesh of the God and a mine map.
  • OPTION B – The Orchard: Walking through the abandoned apple orchard, Hunters find neglected trees, a tool shed with "Gift courtesy of the Browne family" sign, and Clarence's shack containing a slaughterhouse key and mine map marked "well". Clarence pretends to be drunk but will fight with Pig Heads if cornered. If his life is threatened, he reveals everything about Arthur's hideout in the mine.

FOURTH SCENE – The Mine: Following the map, Hunters enter the abandoned mine vein. They encounter either Frank or Clarence (whichever wasn't dealt with earlier) heading to meet Arthur. If both brothers are eliminated, old Martha visits her son instead. The family member receives meat pulled up from a well. If discovered, they use the God's flesh to create Spawn defenders.

FIFTH SCENE – The God Under the Mine: Descending the narrow well, Hunters see light and hear Arthur talking to himself, plus the low moans of the God. Arthur's cave is trapped: boiling water at the well bottom, controllable lights for darkness tactics, and direct access to the God's head. Arthur is insane with obsession for Bardolf's ruin and can create up to 3 Spawn by tearing flesh from the God. If Arthur creates a third Spawn, the God goes mad with pain, causing the mine to collapse. The Hunters must decide Arthur's fate and whether to side with him against Bardolf or stop his revolution.

flags.inspiration: The Three Little Pigs by Joseph Jacobs
flags.setting: Whitehaven, an industrial city in England with coal mines and factories
specialMechanics: Map Token system for mine exploration
